title: silene acaulis subsp. subacaulescens
intro: Silene acaulis subsp. subacaulescens, commonly known as dwarf mountain catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is also known to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Silene acaulis subsp. subacaulescens can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create teas, or by using the plant's extracts in tinctures and salves.
